chunks.title: Success Story: Paul H's Experience
chunks.summary: Paul H shares his journey on an animal-based diet, highlighting his impressive health metrics at age 62. His zero calcium score after two years of eating red meat and taking organ supplements emphasizes the potential benefits of this dietary approach.
chunks.transcript: age 62 animal base for two years calcium heart scan score is zero this is a review from paul h on our beef organs supplement from heart and soil supplements hey dr paul i've been animal based for two years taking your beef organ supplements for a year now and at age 62 i feel unstoppable my annual blood work i got a few months ago showed my total cholesterol of 210 oh my god ldl of 157 which are above the normal range my doctor said i should think about taking a statin so just to make sure he was wrong about that i got a ct calcium heart scan i got the results today my score was wait for it zero that's after two years of eating red meat twice a day a score of zero is also almost unheard of for a 62 year old male my father also had a heart attack died from a failed heart so it is not that i have good quote genes it's the diet and supplements i'm a little worried now i may outlive my kids unless they get on an animal-based diet certainly i hope paul doesn't well there's no good way to really phrase that statement i hope all of paul's family is healthy for as long as they want to live but how cool that paul is an example of somebody living into their 60s eating red meat twice a day taking beef organs from heart and soil supplements thriving calcium score of

chunks.title: Understanding Cholesterol and LDL
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses misconceptions surrounding cholesterol, particularly LDL levels, and their implications for atherosclerosis. He emphasizes the importance of insulin sensitivity and how it relates to overall cardiovascular health.

chunks.title: My Blood Work Journey
chunks.summary: The speaker takes the audience through his blood work history, comparing recent results with previous ones while outlining his dietary practices. He points out how changes in diet can influence various health markers, including cholesterol and insulin levels.

chunks.title: Dietary Approaches and Mood
chunks.summary: The impact of diet on mental health, particularly in children, is addressed. The speaker argues that abandoning nutrient-rich foods like meat and organs can adversely affect mental well-being, contrasting this with the fear of LDL cholesterol.

chunks.title: The Importance of Insulin Sensitivity
chunks.summary: The podcast stresses the critical role of insulin sensitivity in overall health, discussing its connection to blood work and dietary choices. The speaker advocates for the inclusion of whole foods and the avoidance of processed sugars and seed oils.
chunks.transcript: to get post graduate training and i've done lots of reading about research papers there are lots of people who are going to disagree with my assessment of various aspects of my labs so i welcome that conversation if you don't think that my assessment of my labs is correct then put something in the comments or reach out to me on instagram or um yeah so i think that there's a lot to be said about this but i think that the important takeaway from the whole discussion is that labs might look a little different when we're eating a lot of meat a lot of saturated fat from animals and the context is critically important i say that all the time but what i mean by that is that if you are insulin sensitive i think that a lot of things change and many portions of blood work get interpreted by well-meaning intelligent physicians as if they are in a population of people who is massively insulin resistant so yeah for the for the run-of-the-mill physician seeing patients for for a physician for a doctor seeing patients they're just walking off the street if they had some of the blood markers specifically the ldl that i have they might be concerned about their cardiovascular risk but that person is insulin resistant so we'll get into that one moment i think there's a huge hugely important thing to be aware of here because so many of us when we choose to eat an animal-based diet increase the amount of red meat and organs in our diet increase the amount of animal fats with good saturated fats like stearic acid and 18 carbon saturated fat that i've talked about a lot odd chain fatty acids pentadecenoic acid hepatodericanoic acid which are found predominantly in animal foods these are beneficial for humans when we do those things often our ldl cholesterol goes up and a few other things change slightly on our blood work mainstream physicians get worried about that and part of the work that i do is

chunks.title: Evaluating Lipids and Inflammation
chunks.summary: Delving into the nuances of lipid profiles, the speaker discusses metrics like LDL size and particle count. The importance of context when interpreting these markers is emphasized, alongside a claim that red meat is not inflammatory.

chunks.title: Discussion of Hormones and Health
chunks.summary: The speaker examines hormone levels in relation to dietary practices, particularly testosterone and estradiol. The effects of diet on these hormones are explored, with a focus on how a balanced intake can support overall well-being.
chunks.transcript: important so what do i want to talk about here i want to note that with a massive amount quote unquote of fruit and honey in my diet i remain extremely insulin sensitive i don't think anyone would argue this there are people on the keto side who would say honey is bad for you and hopefully i'll be able to have conversations with them in the near future and talk it out in an interesting way but i disagree i think there's lots of good evidence that honey is great for humans i've shown it many times on the podcast previously and people would also say fruit is bad it's going to raise your blood sugar well of course it's going to raise your blood sugar after you eat it postprandially but i think that the real problem that people are pointing at here is are you insulin resistant and no i am not insulin resistant in my case and i think in all cases of homo sapiens eating fruit and eating honey will not make you insulin resistant in any way

chunks.title: Final Thoughts on Diet and Health
chunks.summary: In conclusion, the speaker reiterates the significance of incorporating animal products into one's diet, advocating for organs and meat as vital components for optimal health. He encourages listeners to reevaluate mainstream dietary guidelines.
